failedAssertions.reason: Verification status is 'partial' instead of required 'passed'. The LLM response repair mechanism in validateAndRepairLLMResponse() automatically sets verification.status to 'partial' when it repairs a response, even when the repaired response is mathematically correct. The actual mathematical answer is correct (x=7), steps are pedagogically sound (4 steps with logical flow), but the verification status requirement is not met due to the repair mechanism overriding the actual mathematical verification.

details.testingApproach: Re-run of Round 1 failed assertions after fix implementation. One flow validator subagent tested 3 E2E assertions against the backend API running on localhost:3000 with real OPENROUTER_API_KEY. Tests covered text-only algebra, text-only calculus, and refinement flows.
details.rootCauseAnalysis: VAL-E2E-001 fails because the LLM response repair mechanism in validateAndRepairLLMResponse() automatically sets verification.status to 'partial' when it repairs any response field, regardless of whether the mathematical content is actually correct. The real LLM integration with OPENROUTER_API_KEY is working properly and generating mathematically correct responses (x=7 for 3x-7=14). The symbolic verification engine is also working correctly. The issue is a design flaw in the schema validation layer that conflates 'response was repaired' with 'mathematical verification is uncertain'.
whatWorks: Text-only calculus question (VAL-E2E-002) - Correct derivative (3x^2 + 2), 4 pedagogically sound steps, high confidence
Screenshot-based question (VAL-E2E-003) - Image extraction and LaTeX parsing work correctly
Refinement flow (VAL-E2E-004) - Proper step count variation (3 simpler, 4 default, 5 detailed), all solve same problem correctly
Screenshot refinement with context (VAL-E2E-005) - Stateless design maintains context across requests
Error recovery flow (VAL-E2E-006) - Server remains healthy after malformed requests
Low-confidence image with text fallback (VAL-E2E-007) - System correctly uses clear text over poor image
Verification failure graceful degradation (VAL-E2E-008) - Responses delivered with 'partial' verification
Schema validation (VAL-E2E-009) - All required fields present in responses
Concurrent request handling (VAL-E2E-010) - 5 parallel requests handled correctly
details.improvementsFromRound1: Round 1 had 3 failures (VAL-E2E-001, VAL-E2E-002, VAL-E2E-004) all attributable to mock mode without real API key. After configuring real OPENROUTER_API_KEY, 2 of 3 assertions now pass (VAL-E2E-002, VAL-E2E-004). The remaining failure (VAL-E2E-001) is now due to a design issue in the repair mechanism, not missing API key.
details.recommendation: Fix the validateAndRepairLLMResponse() function to separate response repair from mathematical verification. When the repair mechanism fills in missing fields (e.g., conceptSummary), it should not automatically downgrade verification.status to 'partial'. The verification status should reflect the actual mathematical verification result, not whether schema repair was needed. This is a backend code fix required in backend/server.js.
